item.title: Martinelli's stoppage-time goal sends Brazil into FIFA World Cup Round of 16
item.description: Martinelli's stoppage-time goal sends Brazil into FIFA World Cup Round of 16

item.title: Flying Fijians step up preparations ahead of Wales Test
item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians have wasted no time getting to work in Wales, heading straight into their first training session at Cardiff as preparations intensify ahead of their opening Test this weekend. The Fiji Rugby Union confirms that the squad is now in full game-week mode, with two field sessions scheduled as they fine-tune their preparations for the opening clash against Wales. Adding to
item.content:encoded:
The FIJI Water Flying Fijians have wasted no time getting to work in Wales, heading straight into their first training session at Cardiff as preparations intensify ahead of their opening Test this weekend. The Fiji Rugby Union confirms that the squad is now in full game-week mode, with two field sessions scheduled as they fine-tune their preparations for the opening clash against Wales. Adding to the Fijian presence in Wales, local artists Paradise Rootz, Primavia, and Georgina Ledua will perform before each of Fiji's three tour matches.

The Flying Fijians take on Wales this Saturday at 1.10am before facing England on July 11th at Hill Dickinson Stadium. They will then wrap up their tour against Scotland on July 18th at Murrayfield Stadium.

In other matches this weekend, New Zealand will play France at 7.10pm Saturday in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Celebrations erupt as Ba FC brings BiC Fiji FACT trophy home
item.description: Ba Football’s long-awaited BiC Fiji FACT triumph after 16 years has been celebrated been with jubilant scenes through the streets of Ba Town. The Men in Black returned home with the coveted trophy after defeating Navua in the final, ending the wait for the prestigious title.
item.content:encoded:
Ba Football’s long-awaited BiC Fiji FACT triumph after 16 years has been celebrated been with jubilant scenes through the streets of Ba Town.

The Men in Black returned home with the coveted trophy after defeating Navua in the final, ending the wait for the prestigious title.

Following their victory, players, officials and supporters marched through Ba Town with the trophy, celebrating the historic achievement with hundreds of fans lining the streets.

The BiC Fiji FACT title is Ba FC's first since 2010, ending a 16-year drought and giving their loyal supporters plenty to celebrate.

item.title: Legge proud as Drua Women book home semi-final match
item.description: That is the confident declaration from the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's coach, Mike Legge after his side produced a dominant 50-17 win over the Reds to secure a place in the semi-finals. Legge says he was pleased with the team's overall effort, noting that they had spent the past two to three weeks working on improving their starts, building pressure and maintaining intensity throughout matches.
item.content:encoded:
That is the confident declaration from the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's coach, Mike Legge after his side produced a dominant 50-17 win over the Reds to secure a place in the semi-finals.

Legge says he was pleased with the team's overall effort, noting that they had spent the past two to three weeks working on improving their starts, building pressure and maintaining intensity throughout matches.

He says those improvements were evident on the field, with the playersÂ largely executing the game plan well.

The coach also praised the team's defensive effort, highlighting improvements in tackle technique and line speed, which he says were key positives from the match.

The Drua Women's semi-final opponents in two weeks are yet to be confirmed.

item.title: Canada through to FIFA World Cup last 16 after late Eustáquio winner
item.description: Co-hosts Canada are through to the FIFA World Cup last 16 after edging South Africa 1-0 this morning. Stephen Eustáquio scored the lone goal in the 92nd minute to seal Canada’s place in the next round.
item.content:encoded:
Co-hosts Canada are through to the FIFA World Cup last 16 after edging South Africa 1-0 this morning.

Stephen Eustáquio scored the lone goal in the 92nd minute to seal Canada’s place in the next round.

This is an i ncredible achievement for the co-hosts.

Canada will now face either the Netherlands or Morocco in the last 16.

Netherlands will play Morocco tomorrow at 1pm.

item.title: Drikibau back for Fiji Pearls for Spirit Series
item.description: Former Suva Grammar School student Elina Drikibau returns to the Fiji Pearls netball squad, joining Antolia Nuku, Jima Kete and Vuta Tokaduadua in the midcourt for the Spirit Series. Drikibau made her debut for Fiji in 2022 at just 17 years old during the Nations Cup in Singapore.
item.content:encoded:
Former Suva Grammar School student Elina Drikibau returns to the Fiji Pearls netball squad, joining Antolia Nuku, Jima Kete and Vuta Tokaduadua in the midcourt for the Spirit Series.

Drikibau made her debut for Fiji in 2022 at just 17 years old during the Nations Cup in Singapore.

She hails from Toki Village in Ovalau and shares maternal links to Vanuabalavu.

Esther Dawai, Leehava Saverio, Matila Vocea, and Nina Nakula are the shooters.

In defence, the team features Alisi Naqiri, Elenoa Gukirewa, Kalesi Tawake and Nani Waqaira.

The non-travelling reserves are Robyn Navoka (shooter), Susana Rasaubale (midcourt) and Eseta Waqaira (defence).

The Fiji Pearls will be coached by Michelle Parsons.

The tournament will be held from July 8th to 11th in Sydney, Australia.

item.title: Fijian Drua signs promising QVS fly-half Jone Raibevu to the Development squad
item.description: The Fijian Drua has signed talented Queen Victoria School fly-half and sprinter Jone Raibevu to the Club's Development Squad, marking another success story for the Drua's grassroots talent pathway through the Drua Foundation. Raibevu joins the Development Programme after progressing through the Club's junior development pathway.
item.content:encoded:
The Fijian Drua has signed talented Queen Victoria School fly-half and sprinter Jone Raibevu to the Club's Development Squad, marking another success story for the Drua's grassroots talent pathway through the Drua Foundation.

Raibevu joins the Development Programme after progressing through the Club's junior development pathway.

Drua says he was first identified through the Drua Foundation's talent identification programme in 2024, with his elevation highlighting the Foundation's ongoing commitment to discovering, developing and supporting Fiji's next generation of rugby talent.

They say his signing will help strengthen rugby development across the country.

The Drua Foundation works alongside the Fiji Rugby Union and the Fijian Drua to identify promising young players, provide access to high-quality coaching and development opportunities, and help remove barriers for talented athletes from communities across Fiji.

Raibevu says he was really surprised when he got the call about the opportunity and he was also excited when the Drua committee told him that he would be joining the Drua Development next season.

The Kalabu, Naitasiri youngster first caught national attention during the 2024 Fiji Secondary Schools Rugby Eastern Zone competition with a series of standout performances.

Later that year, he earned selection into the inaugural Fijian Drua Junior Development Programme, where he trained under Drua coaches in a high-performance environment alongside some of Fiji's most promising young players.

Drua says apart from rugby, Raibevu has also excelled in athletics.

The Queen Victoria School student is one of the school's leading sprinters and claimed the prestigious Blue Ribbon title at the Tailevu Zone Athletics Championships, showcasing the speed and athleticism that have made him one of Fiji's most exciting young sporting prospects.

Fijian Drua CEO Jeff Miller welcomed Raibevu to the Club's development system.

He says his signing is another great example of what can be achieved when the Fiji Rugby Union and the Drua invest in identifying and developing young Fijian talent from an early age.

Miller says Raibevu is an exciting young player with a strong skill set, excellent game awareness, and outstanding athletic ability.

Drua says they are delighted to welcome him into their Development Programme and look forward to supporting his growth both on and off the field.

Raibevu becomes the latest young player to graduate through the club's development pathway and will now continue his rugby journey within the Fijian Drua environment as he works towards higher honours in the years ahead.

item.title: England finish top After 2-0 win over Panama in FIFA World Cup clash
item.description: England has finished on top of Group L at the FIFA World Cup after beating Panama 2-0 in their final group match.The match was loc
